attractions.name: Bonaventure Cemetery
attractions.description: Bonaventure Cemetery is a world-renowned historic site located on a scenic bluff overlooking the Wilmington River in Savannah, Georgia. Originally developed on the site of a private plantation, it was established as a public cemetery in 1907 and is famous for its quintessentially Southern Gothic atmosphere. Visitors are drawn to its winding tree-lined pathways, stunning Victorian funerary art, and iconic live oaks draped in Spanish moss (City of Savannah, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 330 Bonaventure Rd., Savannah, GA 31404
attractions.hours: Daily 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (City of Savannah)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, every day of the year (Visit Savannah)
highlights: 'Grave of Little Gracie Watson, featuring a life-sized marble statue ([Gallivanter Tours)', 'Burial site of Academy Award-winning songwriter Johnny Mercer (Visit Savannah)', 'Ornate Gaston Tomb located just inside the main gates (Wikipedia)', "Scenic vistas of the Wilmington River from the cemetery's eastern edge (Savannah First-Timer's Guide)", 'The "Avenue of Oaks," a majestic road lined with centuries-old trees and Spanish moss (Travel Photo Discovery)', 'Extensive collection of Victorian-era sculptures and monuments (Savannah Explored)']
attractions.tips: Apply mosquito repellent before arriving, as the riverside location attracts many insects. Pick up a free map at the Bonaventure Administrative Building near the entrance for self-guided exploration. Respect the site's primary function as a burial ground by maintaining a quiet demeanor (Yelp, Savannah First-Timer's Guide).
attractions.nearby: Wormsloe State Historic Site, Isle of Hope, Thunderbolt Fishing Village, Old Fort Jackson, Savannah Historic District (Savannah First-Timer's Guide, GetYourGuide)

attractions.name: Chippewa Square
attractions.description: Chippewa Square is one of Savannah's most iconic and central squares, laid out in 1815 and named for the Battle of Chippewa in the War of 1812 (Wikipedia)). It is best known as the filming location for the famous bus stop scenes in the movie Forrest Gump (Visit Savannah). The square features the grand James Oglethorpe Monument at its center and is surrounded by historic buildings like the Savannah Theatre and First Baptist Church (Savannah.com).
attractions.address: Intersection of Bull Street and West Perry Street, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Daily: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM (Visit Savannah). Public access is continuous, but regulated activities must end by 9:00 PM.
attractions.admission: Free (City of Savannah). Permit fees apply for private events ($350-$725, Visit Savannah).
highlights: "James Oglethorpe Monument: A bronze statue of Savannah's founder created by Daniel Chester French ([Wikipedia)).", 'Forrest Gump Filming Site: The location where the iconic park bench scenes were filmed (bench since moved to the Savannah History Museum) (Visit Savannah).', 'The Savannah Theatre: Located at 222 Bull Street, it is the oldest theater building in the United States still in use (Savannah.com).', 'First Baptist Church: A historic 1833 Greek Revival church bordering the square (Wikipedia)).', 'Philbrick-Eastman House: A notable Greek Revival mansion completed in 1847 (Savannah.com).', 'Ancient Live Oaks: The square is shaded by towering live oaks draped with iconic Spanish moss (Trolley Tours).']
attractions.tips: While the Forrest Gump bench was a prop and is no longer there, you can see the original at the Savannah History Museum (Visit Savannah). The square is a popular stop for trolley tours, so visit early in the morning or late evening for a quieter experience (Trolley Tours). Photography is best from the north side to capture the Oglethorpe monument with the live oaks (Savannah.com).
attractions.nearby: Colonial Park Cemetery, Wright Square, Madison Square, Independent Presbyterian Church, Savannah History Museum (Wikipedia)).

attractions.name: Columbia Square City of Savannah
attractions.description: Established in 1799, Columbia Square is a serene public space in Savannah's Historic District known for its moss-draped live oaks and the historic Wormsloe Fountain at its center. It is surrounded by architectural gems like the Isaiah Davenport House and the Kehoe House, serving as a peaceful retreat that reflects the city's early preservation efforts Visit Savannah, Savannah Lodging.
attractions.address: Habersham Street (between East State and East York Streets), Savannah, GA 31401 Visit Savannah
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours Savannah Lodging
highlights: "Wormsloe Fountain (a 'rustic' fountain relocated from Wormsloe Plantation in 1970) [Wikipedia)", "Isaiah Davenport House Museum (a restored Federal-style home where Savannah's preservation movement began) Savannah Lodging", 'The Kehoe House (an exquisite Renaissance Revival-style historic inn) Yelp', 'Towering live oak trees adorned with Spanish moss Visit Savannah', 'Abraham Sheftall House and the 17 Hundred 90 Inn Savannah.com']
attractions.tips: The square is a popular quiet spot for lunch or a peaceful stroll away from the more crowded areas like Johnson Square Savannah Explored. Parking is typically enforced Monday–Saturday from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m., but can be found for free on certain streets nearby Yelp. Visit during the fall for the most pleasant temperatures Yelp.
attractions.nearby: Davenport House Museum, Webb Military Museum, Kehoe House, 17 Hundred 90 Inn, Owens-Thomas House & Slave Quarters, Colonial Park Cemetery Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Davenport House Museum
attractions.description: The Davenport House Museum is a beautifully preserved Federal-style home built in 1820 by master builder Isaiah Davenport. It is famously known as the site that sparked Savannah's historic preservation movement in 1955 when it was saved from demolition by the Historic Savannah Foundation. Today, it offers an immersive look into 19th-century domestic life, including the stories of the Davenport family and the enslaved people who lived and worked on the property. Davenport House Museum, Visit Savannah
attractions.address: 323 E. Broughton Street (Museum Shop & Entrance)
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Thursday: 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Friday: 11:00 AM - 3:30 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Sunday: Closed. Davenport House Museum
attractions.admission: Adults: $15, Students (18-21): $12, Children (6-17): $10, Children under 6: FREE. Discounts for Seniors, Military, and National Trust members are available at the door. Davenport House Museum
highlights: 'Authentic Federal-style architecture built in 1820 by master builder Isaiah Davenport.', 'The Urban Enslaved Exhibition, which explores the lives of the 13 enslaved people who lived at the home.', 'A stunning courtyard garden redesigned by renowned landscape designer Penelope Hobhouse.', 'Exquisitely restored interiors featuring period furnishings and a signature hanging staircase.', "The 'Old Chan Magic' exhibition highlighting the history of Chinese families in Savannah.", "Recognition as the 'birthplace' of Savannah's historic preservation movement. [Davenport House Museum, Old Town Trolley Tours"]
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to book guided tour times online in advance to guarantee a spot. The museum entrance is located through the Historic Kennedy Pharmacy at the corner of Broughton and Habersham Streets. Be aware that the house tour involves navigating three sets of curved stairs and does not have an elevator. Davenport House Museum
attractions.nearby: Columbia Square, Owens-Thomas House & Slave Quarters, Andrew Low House, Ships of the Sea Maritime Museum, Telfair Academy. Visit Savannah, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Ellis Square
attractions.description: Ellis Square is one of Savannah's original 22 squares, famously restored in 2010 after being used as a parking garage for decades. It serves as a vibrant urban plaza featuring an interactive splash fountain, a bronze statue of songwriter Johnny Mercer, and a modern visitor center. Located at the western end of City Market, it is a popular gathering spot for both locals and tourists to relax and enjoy the lively atmosphere of the historic district.
attractions.address: Barnard and West Congress Streets (200 W Congress St)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (Fountains typically active until 10:00 PM)
highlights: ["Interactive 'splash pad' dancing fountain with evening light displays", 'Bronze statue of Savannah native and legendary songwriter Johnny Mercer', 'Modern Visitor Information Center with glass walls and public restrooms', 'Life-size outdoor chess set and shaded seating areas', 'Convenient underground parking garage directly beneath the square', 'Direct proximity to the shops, restaurants, and entertainment of City Market']
attractions.tips: The fountains are a great way for children to cool off during hot Savannah afternoons, so consider bringing a towel. For a colorful display, visit after sunset when the fountain jets are illuminated with multi-colored lights. The square is an excellent home base for exploring City Market, but be aware it offers less shade than other more traditional, oak-covered squares in the city.
attractions.nearby: City Market, Franklin Square, First African Baptist Church, The Lady and Sons Restaurant, Telfair Museum, Jepson Center for the Arts

attractions.name: Factor's Walk (also Factors Walk or Factors' Walk)
attractions.category: Landmark / Historic District Wikipedia, Visit Savannah
attractions.description: Factor's Walk is a unique historic district in Savannah consisting of a series of elevated iron and concrete walkways and cobblestone ramps that bridge the city's bluff-top streets with the riverfront warehouses. Originally the hub of the 19th-century cotton industry, it was named for the "factors" or brokers who determined the value of cotton from their upper-story offices. Today, the area is a picturesque promenade lined with boutique shops, art galleries, cafes, and historic inns, offering a multi-leveled glimpse into Savannah's commercial heritage. Savannah.com, Savannah First-Timer's Guide
attractions.address: Factors Walk (between E. Bay St. and E. River St.), Savannah, GA 31401 Visit Savannah
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Individual businesses like shops and restaurants have varying hours, typically 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM) Explore Georgia, Yelp
highlights: 'Historic iron pedestrian bridges connecting Bay Street to the upper floors of former cotton warehouses [Savannah.com', "The Cluskey Vaults, a series of 1840s arched brick structures built into the embankment Savannah First-Timer's Guide", 'Original ballast-stone streets and ramps repurposed from 19th-century ship ballast Wikipedia', "The Old Savannah Cotton Exchange Building, featuring its iconic winged lion statue Savannah First-Timer's Guide", 'Graveface Museum, a unique attraction specializing in oddities and true crime memorabilia Visit Savannah', 'Intricate multi-level architecture including the "Stone Stairs of Death" leading to River Street Tripadvisor']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the original cobblestones and ballast-stone ramps are notoriously uneven and difficult to traverse. Be prepared for steep climbs if using the stairs, such as the famous 'Stone Stairs of Death.' For the best views of the multi-leveled architecture, view the walk from the Abercorn or Drayton Street ramps. Savannah First-Timer's Guide, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: River Street, Savannah City Hall, Emmet Park, Old Cotton Exchange, Plant Riverside District Savannah.com, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Forsyth Park City of Savannah
attractions.description: Forsyth Park is the oldest and largest public park in Savannah, spanning 30 acres in the heart of the city's Historic District. Established in the 1840s and named after Governor John Forsyth, it is famous for its iconic Parisian-inspired fountain, lush live oaks draped in Spanish moss, and as a hub for social, cultural, and recreational activities. City of Savannah, Visit Savannah
attractions.address: 10 Whitaker St., Savannah, GA 31401 Explore Georgia, ForsythPark.com
attractions.hours: Daily 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM; Fragrant Garden open Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 2:00 PM Explore Georgia, Go South! Savannah
highlights: 'Iconic Forsyth Fountain (installed 1858) [Savannah.com', 'Fragrant Garden (Garden of Fragrance) City of Savannah', "Forsyth Farmers' Market (Saturdays 9 AM
1 PM) Forsyth Farmers' Market", 'Civil War Memorial and Spanish-American War Monument The Cultural Landscape Foundation', "Two children's playgrounds and a splash pad Savannah.com"]
attractions.tips: Visit the Farmers' Market on Saturday mornings for local treats. Note that the Fragrant Garden has limited weekday hours. Use the free 'Dot' shuttle for easy transport to the park. Visit Savannah, Go South! Savannah
attractions.nearby: Mercer-Williams House, Monterey Square, and Brighter Day Natural Foods Savannah.com

attractions.name: Fort Pulaski National Monument (NPS)
attractions.description: Fort Pulaski National Monument preserves a 19th-century masonry fort that served as a turning point in military history when Union rifled cannons breached its walls in 1862 (NPS). Beyond the historic fortifications, the site encompasses over 5,000 acres of pristine salt marsh and coastal landscape on Cockspur and McQueens Islands (NPS).
attractions.address: 101 Fort Pulaski Road (NPS)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Visitor Center/Park; Fort closes at 4:30 PM) (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily, except New Year's Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas (NPS)
highlights: 'Touring the historic 19th-century masonry fort and its moat ([NPS)', 'Viewing the impacts of Union rifled cannon fire on the fort walls (Wikipedia)', 'Hiking the Lighthouse Overlook Trail for views of the Cockspur Island Lighthouse (NPS)', 'Observing diverse wildlife and bird species in the salt marshes (NPS)', 'Participating in ranger-led programs and historic weapons demonstrations (NPS)', "Exploring the park's museum and visitor center exhibits (NPS)"]
attractions.tips: The park is cashless; bring a credit or debit card for the entrance fee (NPS). Arrive before 4:30 PM as that is the final daily entry time for the historic fort (NPS). Pets are allowed on the grounds but are prohibited inside the visitor center and other buildings (NPS).
attractions.nearby: Tybee Island, Cockspur Island Lighthouse, Savannah Historic District, McQueens Island Trail (Wikipedia, NPS)

attractions.name: Georgia State Railroad Museum
attractions.description: The Georgia State Railroad Museum is a historic site featuring the most complete antebellum railroad repair facility buildings in the United States. Located at the former Central of Georgia Railway Savannah Shops and Terminal Facilities, the museum offers interactive experiences including touring historic railcars, operating a handcar, and taking guided site tours by train. CHS, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 655 Louisville Road, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Daily 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day CHS
attractions.admission: $20 per adult, $14 per child (ages 3-12); Families with SNAP EBT benefits receive FREE admission CHS
highlights: ['Historic roundhouse with an operating 85-foot turntable', 'The oldest portable steam engine in the United States', 'Interactive handcar experiences and guided site tours by train', 'Collection of historic steam and diesel locomotives and rolling stock', 'Massive 125-foot brick smokestack and historic shop buildings']
attractions.tips: Check the daily schedule for train ride times; admission includes access to the STEAM Center (9am-3pm). The museum includes outdoor sections and may close during inclement weather. CHS
attractions.nearby: Savannah Children's Museum, Savannah History Museum, Battlefield Memorial Park, SCAD Museum of Art Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Green-Meldrim House
attractions.description: The Green-Meldrim House is one of the American South's finest examples of Gothic Revival architecture, designated as a National Historic Landmark in 1976. Built in 1853, it notably served as the headquarters for General William Tecumseh Sherman during the Civil War following his March to the Sea. Visit Savannah, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 14 West Macon Street
attractions.hours: Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM, Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Last tour at 3:30 PM). Green-Meldrim House
attractions.admission: Adults: $15, Seniors 65+: $13.50, Students (ages 7+): $12, Children under 7: Free. Private tours: $100 for 1-4 people, $25 per additional person. Green-Meldrim House
highlights: ['Stunning example of Gothic Revival architecture designed by John S. Norris.', "Served as General Sherman's headquarters during the Civil War.", 'Features an ornate interior dome and intricate plasterwork.', "Located on the historic Madison Square next to St. John's Church.", 'Original architectural details including triple front doors and silver-plated hardware.', "Home to 'Sophie’s Shop,' offering unique books and gifts."]
attractions.tips: Tickets for daily tours are purchased on-site; no advance sales for regular tours. The house is not ADA compliant due to its historic 1850 construction. Non-flash photography is permitted for personal use. Green-Meldrim House
attractions.nearby: Madison Square, St. John's Episcopal Church, E. Shaver Booksellers, Andrew Low House, Gryphon Tea Room, Forsyth Park. Green-Meldrim House

attractions.name: Historic River Street
attractions.category: Landmark, Entertainment, Shopping, Dining Savannah's Waterfront
attractions.description: Historic River Street is a world-famous waterfront destination featuring over 200 years of history preserved in its iconic cobblestone streets and former cotton warehouses. Today, the bustling promenade offers a vibrant mix of more than 75 shops, galleries, and restaurants, providing a front-row seat to massive cargo ships navigating the Savannah River. Savannah's Waterfront, Visit Savannah
attractions.address: River St, Savannah, GA 31401 Tripadvisor
attractions.hours: Individual business hours vary; most shops open daily 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM; restaurants and bars often stay open until 11:00 PM or later Savannah's Waterfront
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week Savannah's Waterfront
highlights: "John P. Rousakis Riverfront Plaza: A half-mile pedestrian promenade along the river [Savannah's Waterfront", "Plant Riverside District: A premier entertainment hub with luxury dining and nightlife Savannah's Waterfront", 'River Street Market Place: An open-air market replicating 19th-century trade sheds River Street Market Place', "African American Monument: A tribute sculpture featuring an inscription by Maya Angelou Savannah's Waterfront", "Waving Girl Statue: A historic monument dedicated to Florence Martus Savannah's Waterfront", "Savannah Belles Ferry: Free ferry service connecting the waterfront to Hutchinson Island Savannah's Waterfront"]
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes for the historic cobblestone streets, use the public elevators to avoid the 'Stone Stairs of Death,' and keep your alcoholic beverage in a plastic 'to-go' cup to enjoy the open-container allowance. Visit Savannah, Savannah's Waterfront
attractions.nearby: City Hall, Factors Walk, Old Cotton Exchange, Franklin Square, Emmet Park Savannah's Waterfront

attractions.name: Johnson Square
attractions.description: Established in 1733, Johnson Square is the oldest and largest of Savannah's 22 historic squares and serves as the center of the city's financial district (Wikipedia)). It features two fountains, a sundial, and a prominent obelisk monument honoring Revolutionary War hero General Nathanael Greene (Visit Savannah). The square is a hub of civic life and is surrounded by notable historic buildings, including City Hall and Christ Church Episcopal (Gallivanter Tours).
attractions.address: Bull St. & St. Julian St., Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (For 91 Days)
highlights: 'The Nathanael Greene Monument, an 1825 obelisk where the general is interred ([Wikipedia))', 'Two ornate fountains and a historic sundial dedicated to Colonel William Bull (Visit Savannah)', "Christ Church Episcopal, known as the 'Mother Church of Georgia' (Wikipedia))", "Savannah's first 'skyscraper', the Johnson Square Business Center (Wikipedia))", 'Site of the first flag of independence raised in the South in 1860 (Wikipedia))', 'The oldest building on the square, the 1824 Ann Hamilton House (Wikipedia))']
attractions.tips: The square is a popular spot for office workers to eat lunch and often hosts community events and live music (La Dolce Savannah). It is a prime starting point for walking tours of the historic district (Southern Belle Vacation Rentals). Ensure you look for the sundial which marks the original city plan (Visit Savannah).
attractions.nearby: City Hall, Ellis Square, Wright Square, City Market, Olde Pink House (Wikipedia), Savannah.com)

attractions.name: Juliette Gordon Low Birthplace Museum Official Website
attractions.category: Museum / National Historic Landmark Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: The Juliette Gordon Low Birthplace is a beautifully restored Federal-style mansion that serves as the first National Historic Landmark in Savannah. It is the childhood home of Juliette 'Daisy' Gordon Low, the visionary founder of the Girl Scouts of the USA, and today stands as a museum celebrating her life and the movement she launched. Official Website, Visit Savannah
attractions.address: 10 East Oglethorpe Ave, Savannah, GA 31401 Official Website, Explore Georgia
attractions.hours: Open 7 days a week: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Last tour typically begins at 3:50 PM). Limited tour hours on Wednesdays (10:00 AM – 12:00 PM per some listings; check official calendar). Museum store is open until 5:00 PM. Official Website, Visit Savannah
attractions.admission: $15 Adult; $12 Senior (60+), Military, or Student (ages 5–21); $10 Registered Girl Scout (Adults and Students); Free for children 4 and under. Girl Scout Troop Experience: $28 per person (minimum 8 participants). Official Website
highlights: 'First National Historic Landmark in Savannah, designated in 1965 [Official Website', 'Site of the historic 1912 phone call where Juliette Low announced the founding of the Girl Scouts Official Website', 'Regency-style architecture featuring two distinctive semi-circular bays at the rear Wikipedia', 'Extensive collection of original Gordon family furnishings and artwork created by Juliette Low herself Official Website', "Formal Victorian parterre garden designed in 1956 by Savannah's first female landscape architect, Clermont Lee Official Website", 'Owned and operated by Girl Scouts of the USA for over 70 years Girl Scouts of the USA']
attractions.tips: Guided tours are the only way to see the interior of the house, so online reservations are highly recommended as tickets often sell out. Visitors are welcome to browse the garden independently before or after their tour. The museum is one of the few historic house museums in Savannah with elevator accessibility to all floors. Official Website, Visit Savannah
attractions.nearby: Andrew Low House, Girl Scout First Headquarters, Colonial Park Cemetery, Wright Square, and the Historic Savannah District. Wikipedia, Old Town Trolley Tours

attractions.name: Lafayette Square
attractions.description: Established in 1837 and named for the Marquis de Lafayette, this historic square is one of Savannah's most picturesque public spaces. It serves as a central hub in the Historic District, featuring the ornate Semiquincentenary Fountain and surrounded by significant landmarks like the Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist and the Andrew Low House. (City of Savannah, Savannah.com)
attractions.address: Abercorn St & E Charlton St, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Monday-Sunday) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Semiquincentenary Fountain, a memorial gift from the Colonial Dames of America ([Wikipedia))', 'The Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist, featuring the highest twin steeples in downtown Savannah (Savannah.com)', 'Andrew Low House, a historic museum and former home of Girl Scouts founder Juliette Gordon Low (Official Savannah Guide)', 'Hamilton-Turner House, the first residence in Savannah to have electricity (Savannah.com)', "Flannery O'Connor Childhood Home, a museum dedicated to the famous Southern author (Expedia)", "Mary Musgrove Historical Marker, honoring the interpreter for Georgia's founder (Official Savannah Guide)"]
attractions.tips: For the best photos, stand on the south side of the fountain to capture the Cathedral Basilica in the background. Visitors on St. Patrick's Day can see the fountain's water dyed green for the city's famous celebration. (Savannah First-Timer's Guide, Savannah.com)
attractions.nearby: Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist, Andrew Low House, Flannery O'Connor Childhood Home, Hamilton-Turner Inn, Colonial Park Cemetery, Madison Square (Expedia, Savannah.com)

attractions.name: Madison Square
attractions.description: Laid out in 1837 and named for President James Madison, this historic public square is a central feature of Savannah's Historic Landmark District. It is famously home to the William Jasper Monument and is surrounded by architectural gems like the Sorrel-Weed House and the Green-Meldrim House. (Wikipedia), Visit Savannah)
attractions.address: Bull and Macon Streets, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Forsyth Park/Savannah Parks)
highlights: 'William Jasper Monument, a 15-foot bronze statue memorializing a Revolutionary War hero ([Visit Savannah)', "Historic cannon markers from the Savannah Armory indicating the start of Georgia's first highways (Wikipedia))", 'Surrounding Gothic, Romanesque, and Greek Revival architecture including the Sorrel-Weed House (Tripadvisor)', 'Proximity to the Savannah College of Art and Design (SCAD) and its boutique shops like shopSCAD (Visit Savannah)', "Beautiful live oaks and walking paths typical of Savannah's Oglethorpe Plan (Tripadvisor)", 'Monument marking the center of British resistance during the Siege of Savannah (Wikipedia))']
attractions.tips: As a popular stop on trolley tours, visit in the early morning or late afternoon for a more peaceful experience. The square is an ideal spot for photography of the Jasper Monument and surrounding historic homes. (Old Town Trolley Tours, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Sorrel-Weed House, Green-Meldrim House, St. John's Episcopal Church, shopSCAD, SCADstory, The Public Kitchen & Bar, Artillery Bar (Visit Savannah, Wikipedia))

attractions.name: Mercer Williams House Museum
attractions.description: The Mercer Williams House Museum is a historic Italianate mansion designed by John S. Norris and completed in 1868. Famous as the primary setting for the book and film "Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil," the home was meticulously restored by preservationist Jim Williams in 1969. Today, it showcases a world-class collection of 18th and 19th-century antiques, artwork, and Chinese export porcelain within its elegantly preserved interiors. Mercer Williams House Museum, Savannah.com
attractions.address: 429 Bull Street, Savannah, GA 31401 (Entrance located behind the main house at 430 Whitaker Street)
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 10:00 am - 5:00 pm (First tour at 10:30 am, last tour at 4:10 pm); Sunday: 11:30 am - 5:00 pm (First tour at 12:00 pm, last tour at 4:00 pm) Mercer Williams House Museum
attractions.days_open: Open 7 days a week (Closed on St. Patrick’s Day; Closed on Wednesdays during the month of February) Mercer Williams House Museum
attractions.admission: Adult ticket: $14.95; Students and Active Military (with ID): $12.00; Children 6 and under: Free (All prices subject to 7% Georgia Sales Tax) Mercer Williams House Museum
highlights: 'Designed by renowned New York architect John S. Norris with a mix of Renaissance Revival and Italianate styles. [Mercer Williams House Museum', "Restored by Jim Williams, one of Savannah's most dedicated preservationists, in a two-year project starting in 1969. Old Town Trolley Tours", 'Features an original 1868 interior dome with stained glass and 15-foot ceilings with floor-length windows. Mercer Williams House Museum', 'Houses a private collection of Gilbert Stuart portraits and 18th-century Chinese export porcelain. Mercer Williams House Museum', 'Constructed with unique "Philadelphia Red" bricks and retains its original English ceramic tile in the 60-foot entrance hall. Mercer Williams House Museum', 'Infamous as the site of the events depicted in John Berendt\'s "Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil." Gallivanter Tours']
attractions.tips: No photography or video is permitted inside the house or garden; tours are offered on a first-come, first-served basis. The museum is not handicap accessible as it is a historic property, and tours only include the first floor as the upper levels remain a private residence. Mercer Williams House Museum, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Monterey Square, Casimir Pulaski Monument, Temple Mickve Israel, Madison Square, Forsyth Park, Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist Visit Savannah, Expedia

attractions.name: Monterey Square (Wikipedia))
attractions.description: Monterey Square was laid out in 1847 to commemorate the 1846 Battle of Monterey during the Mexican-American War. Often described as Savannah's most picturesque square, it features a 55-foot monument dedicated to Revolutionary War hero General Casimir Pulaski. The square is surrounded by historic landmarks including the Mercer-Williams House and Congregation Mickve Israel. (Savannah.com, Wikipedia))
attractions.address: Bull Street Between Taylor and Gordon Streets, Savannah, GA 31401-4913 (Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 'Casimir Pulaski Monument, a 55-foot Italian marble memorial ([Savannah.com)', "The Mercer-Williams House, famously featured in 'Midnight in the Garden of Good and Evil' (Wikipedia))", 'Congregation Mickve Israel, one of the few Gothic Revival synagogues in the U.S. (Savannah.com)', 'Lush landscaping and mature live oak trees draped in Spanish moss (Expedia)', 'Historic brick walkways and shaded benches for visitors (Expedia)']
attractions.tips: As one of the most picturesque squares, it is an ideal spot for photography and is a favorite stop on historical walking tours. It is just two blocks north of Forsyth Park, making it a convenient stop during a walk through the historic district. (Tripadvisor, Savannah.com)
attractions.nearby: Forsyth Park, Mercer-Williams House Museum, Congregation Mickve Israel, Madison Square, Calhoun Square (Tripadvisor, Savannah.com)

attractions.name: Old Fort Jackson (Fort James Jackson)
attractions.description: Old Fort Jackson is Georgia's oldest standing brick fortification and a National Historic Landmark located on the Savannah River. Constructed between 1808 and 1812, it served as a key coastal defense during the War of 1812 and later as the headquarters for Confederate river defenses during the Civil War. (Coastal Heritage Society, American Battlefield Trust)
attractions.address: 1 Fort Jackson Road
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Tuesday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Wednesday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Thursday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Friday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Saturday: 9:00am - 4:00pm, Sunday: 9:00am - 4:00pm (Coastal Heritage Society)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas, and New Year’s Day) (Coastal Heritage Society)
attractions.admission: $15 per adult, $10 per child (ages 3-12). Free for children under 3. Discounted $21 "See 3" tickets available for multiple Coastal Heritage Society sites. (Coastal Heritage Society, Savannah Explored)
highlights: 'Daily cannon firing demonstrations at 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM ([Coastal Heritage Society)', 'Interactive military history programs with costumed interpreters (Coastal Heritage Society)', 'Well-preserved Second System coastal defense architecture and 9-inch Dahlgren cannon (Expedia)', 'Scenic views of the Savannah River and opportunities for wildlife spotting (Expedia)', 'Historical exhibits and casemates detailing the lives of soldiers (Coastal Heritage Society)']
attractions.tips: Plan your visit around the 11 AM or 2 PM cannon firings for the full experience. Consider the "See 3" pass if you plan to visit other local history museums to save on admission. Wear comfortable shoes as the grounds are historic and uneven. (Coastal Heritage Society, Savannah Explored)
attractions.nearby: Fort Pulaski National Monument, Georgia State Railroad Museum, Savannah History Museum, Pin Point Heritage Museum, Savannah Children's Museum (American Battlefield Trust, Savannah Explored)

attractions.name: Old Savannah Tours
attractions.description: Old Savannah Tours offers a captivating 75-minute, non-stop overview journey through the heart of Savannah aboard custom open-air trolleys. The fully narrated tour covers over 270 years of history, featuring more than 75 fascinating points of interest including iconic landmarks and hidden gems. Led by certified and entertaining guides, it provides a seamless sightseeing experience that brings the city's storied past to life. Source: Official Website
attractions.address: 217 West Boundary Street
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Last tour times vary; check availability calendar) Source: Visit Savannah
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on St. Patrick's Day, March 17)
attractions.admission: Adult (Ages 13+): $38.00 (online rate with 5% discount), Child (Ages 4-12): $19.00 (online rate with 5% discount). Reservations are required. Source: Official Website
highlights: ["75-minute non-stop sightseeing tour of Savannah's historic sites", 'Certified, experienced, and entertaining local tour guides', 'Custom-built, open-air trolleys with cushioned seating for optimal photography', 'Over 75 fascinating points of interest covering 270+ years of history', 'Live narration featuring historical tales, exclusive insights, and hidden gems', 'Free parking available for ticket holders at the Welcome Center']
attractions.tips: Reservations are required and tickets are non-refundable, though they can be rescheduled up to 24 hours in advance. Arrive at least 20 minutes prior to your ticketed tour time for check-in at the Welcome Center. Free parking is available but space is limited, so arrive early. Source: Official Website
attractions.nearby: Savannah History Museum, Savannah Children’s Museum, Ships of the Sea Maritime Museum, City Market, and the Georgia State Railroad Museum. Source: Old Town Trolley

attractions.name: Old Town Trolley Tours of Savannah
attractions.description: Old Town Trolley Tours of Savannah offers a comprehensive hop-on, hop-off sightseeing experience covering nine miles of the city's Historic and Victorian Districts. With 16 convenient stops and live, expert narration, it allows visitors to explore over 100 points of interest at their own pace. Old Town Trolley Website
attractions.address: 234 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (August - March); 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM (April - June) Old Town Trolley Schedule
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed on St. Patrick's Day) Old Town Trolley Schedule
attractions.admission: 1-Day Tour: Adult $44.60, Child $22.75; 2-Day Tour: Adult $77.90 Old Town Trolley Tickets
highlights: ['16 convenient stops near major landmarks', '100% live, real-time narration by expert guides', 'Unlimited all-day hop-on, hop-off privileges', 'Included tasting experience at Old Savannah Distillery', 'Frequent service with departures approximately every 20 minutes', 'Coverage of 22 historic squares and the Victorian District']
attractions.tips: Start your journey at Stop #1 for the best overview and convenient parking. Utilize the 2-day pass for better value if exploring multiple museums, and download the Trolley Tracker Map for real-time vehicle locations. Old Town Trolley Map
attractions.nearby: City Market, Forsyth Park, River Street, Cathedral of St. John the Baptist, American Prohibition Museum Old Town Trolley Map

attractions.name: Owens-Thomas House & Slave Quarters Telfair Museums
attractions.description: The Owens-Thomas House & Slave Quarters is a premier example of Regency architecture in America, designed by William Jay and completed in 1819. The museum offers a comprehensive perspective on the antebellum South by exploring the lives of both the wealthy Owens family and the enslaved people who lived and labored on the property. Telfair Museums
attractions.address: 124 Abercorn St., Savannah, GA 31401 Telfair Museums
attractions.hours: Tuesday-Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Monday: CLOSED. Note: Tours are given every 15 minutes, with the last tour starting at 4:00 PM. Telfair Museums
attractions.admission: Adults: $30; Seniors (65+): $27; Active Military: $27; Students (13-25): $20; Children (6-12): $10; Children (5 & under): Free; Telfair Members: Free Telfair Museums Admission
attractions.phone: 912.790.8800 (Main) or 912.790.8889 (Site specific) Telfair Museums, Visit Savannah
highlights: 'Regency-style mansion designed by architect William Jay. [Telfair Museums', 'The only intact urban slave quarters open to the public in Savannah. Telfair Museums', 'One of the first homes in the United States to feature indoor plumbing and running water. Official Savannah Guide', 'Original "haint blue" paint preserved in the slave quarters. Telfair Museums', 'Beautiful formal parterre garden located between the mansion and carriage house. Telfair Museums', 'Guided tours that focus on the complex history of slavery and freedom. Telfair Museums']
attractions.tips: Tickets are valid for seven days and include admission to the Jepson Center and Telfair Academy. Due to the building's historic nature, there is no elevator access and visitors must use original steep staircases. Visitors must check in at the onsite ticket booth even if tickets were purchased online or at another site. Telfair Museums, Telfair Museums Admission
attractions.nearby: Oglethorpe Square, Telfair Academy, Jepson Center, Davenport House Museum, Colonial Park Cemetery Telfair Museums, Google Maps

attractions.name: Reynolds Square Wikipedia)
attractions.description: Reynolds Square is one of the 22 historic squares of Savannah, laid out in 1734 and named for Captain John Reynolds, the first royal governor of Georgia. Located in the city's northernmost row of squares, it serves as a serene public space surrounded by significant historic architecture and lush greenery. Wikipedia), Visit Savannah
attractions.address: Abercorn Street (between E. Bryan and E. Congress Streets), Savannah, GA 31401 City of Savannah, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours Access Travel
highlights: 'Bronze statue of John Wesley, the founder of Methodism, by sculptor Marshall Daugherty [Wikipedia)', 'The Olde Pink House (Habersham House), the oldest building on the square dating to 1771 Wikipedia)', 'The Lucas Theatre, a historic venue built in 1921 for silent movies Savannah Lodging', 'The Oliver Sturges House, a prominent Georgian-style townhouse Expedia', 'Former site of the Filature, an early 18th-century silk industry effort Wikipedia)', 'Proximity to the Savannah River and historic downtown district Expedia']
attractions.tips: The square is a popular photo spot, especially with the John Wesley statue and the Olde Pink House. It is also a great starting point for exploring the downtown historic district. Visitors should note that while the park is open 24/7, surrounding attractions like the Lucas Theatre have their own specific schedules. Tripadvisor, Expedia
attractions.nearby: Savannah River, Johnson Square, Warren Square, Oglethorpe Square, Leopold's Ice Cream, Owens-Thomas House Wikipedia), Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Savannah City Market Official Website
attractions.category: Shopping, Dining, and Entertainment District Visit Savannah
attractions.description: Established in 1755, City Market is a historic four-block open-air marketplace in the heart of downtown Savannah. Once the city's central hub for groceries and services, it has been restored into a vibrant destination featuring popular restaurants, unique shops, art galleries, and lively entertainment venues. Visit Savannah, Official Website
attractions.address: 219 W. Bryan Street, Savannah, GA 31401 Official Website
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Note: Individual business hours may vary) Joe Coffee, Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Free (Entry to the open-air market is free; specific attractions like the American Prohibition Museum require tickets) Old Town Trolley Tours, Savannah City Market
highlights: "American Prohibition Museum and its Zagat-rated speakeasy, Congress Street Up [Savannah First-Timer's Guide", "Art Center on the second floor, housing dozens of active artists' studios Official Website", 'Outdoor courtyard with live music and seasonal events Old Town Trolley Tours', 'Eclectic mix of boutiques, souvenir shops, and local art galleries Artsy Chow Roamer', 'A variety of dining options, from casual pizza to upscale restaurants like Belford’s Wikipedia)', 'Year-round festivities including the Art & Soul Music Festival and holiday celebrations Visit Savannah']
attractions.tips: Visit the second-floor Art Center to meet local artists in their studios. Grab a to-go cocktail or slushy to enjoy while listening to live music in the courtyard. If it’s raining or too hot, the American Prohibition Museum is an excellent indoor activity. Savannah First-Timer's Guide
attractions.nearby: Ellis Square, Franklin Square, Telfair Academy, Jepson Center for the Arts, River Street, and the First African Baptist Church Tripadvisor, Old Town Trolley Tours

attractions.name: Savannah Historic District
attractions.description: The Savannah Historic District is one of the largest urban National Historic Landmark Districts in the United States, preserving the original 1733 town plan designed by James Oglethorpe. The district features 22 picturesque squares, cobblestone streets, and over 1,100 historic buildings representing various architectural styles. Wikipedia), Visit Savannah
attractions.address: 301 Martin Luther King Jr Blvd (Visitor Information Center)
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Visitor Center hours). Visit Savannah
attractions.days_open: 363 days a year (Closed Christmas Day and New Year's Day). Visit Savannah
attractions.admission: Free; individual attractions and museums within the district have varying fees. Visit Savannah, Old Town Trolley Tours
highlights: ['22 Historic Squares and Parks', 'River Street Waterfront and Promenade', 'Forsyth Park and its iconic Fountain', 'Cathedral Basilica of St. John the Baptist', 'City Market Art and Dining Hub', 'Juliette Gordon Low Birthplace']
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the MLK Visitor Center for free maps and information. The district is very walkable, but trolley tours are a popular way to see major landmarks without the stress of parking. TourPass, Old Town Trolley Tours
attractions.nearby: Savannah Victorian Historic District, Bonaventure Cemetery, Old Fort Jackson, Tybee Island. Wikipedia, Old Town Trolley Tours

attractions.name: Telfair Square
attractions.description: Telfair Square is a historic public park in Savannah, Georgia, originally established in 1733 as St. James Square. It was renamed in 1883 to honor the Telfair family, prominent philanthropists who significantly contributed to the city's cultural development (Wikipedia). Today, the square serves as a cultural epicenter, surrounded by major art institutions and historic architecture (Staci Donegan Real Estate).
attractions.address: Barnard St & W President St, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Airial Travel)
highlights: 'Surrounded by the Telfair Academy and the Jepson Center for the Arts ([Savannah.com)', "Home to the Trinity United Methodist Church, the city's oldest Methodist congregation (Savannah.com)", 'Features unique monuments including a tribute to the Girl Scouts of the USA and a chambered nautilus sculpture (Wikipedia)', 'The only Savannah square named in honor of a family rather than an individual (Wikipedia)', "Contains the Girl Scouts Garden, landscaped in the shape of the organization's trefoil logo (Staci Donegan Real Estate)"]
attractions.tips: Visit during weekday mornings for a more peaceful experience with fewer crowds (Airial Travel). While the square itself is free to enjoy 24/7, visiting during daylight hours is recommended to fully appreciate the monuments and surrounding architecture (Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Telfair Academy, Jepson Center for the Arts, Trinity United Methodist Church, Juliette Gordon Low Birthplace, City Market (Savannah.com)

attractions.name: The Old Squares of Savannah Walking Tour (by WalknTours)
attractions.description: The Old Squares of Savannah Walking Tour is a smartphone-guided solo experience that leads visitors through the city's historic district at their own pace. Using GPS-triggered audio narration, the tour shares stories of the city's founding, its famous residents like James Oglethorpe and Eli Whitney, and iconic landmarks such as the Forrest Gump bench site. WalknTours, Viator
attractions.address: Johnson Square, Savannah, GA 31401, USA (Starting Point) Viator
attractions.hours: Available 24/7 (App-based) WalknTours
attractions.admission: Starting from $5.99 - $9.75 per person (Self-guided audio tour) WalknTours, Viator
highlights: "Johnson Square (Savannah's first square) [WalknTours", 'Chippewa Square (Forrest Gump bench site) WalknTours', 'Birthplace of Juliette Gordon Low (Girl Scouts founder) WalknTours', 'Telfair Academy (Oldest art museum in the South) WalknTours', "Tomochichi's Memorial and the grave of Nathanael Greene WalknTours", 'The Olde Pink House and historic mansions WalknTours']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the tour covers cobblestone streets and approximately 1-2 miles of walking. Start early in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the Georgia heat, and bring a water bottle for the journey. SelfGuidedWalk
attractions.nearby: City Market, River Street, Forsyth Park, The Pirate's House Visit Savannah

attractions.name: Warren Square
attractions.description: Warren Square is a tranquil public park located in Savannah's northernmost row of squares, established in 1791 and named after Revolutionary War hero General Joseph Warren. It is characterized by its quiet residential charm, featuring a lush green lawn, mature live oaks draped in Spanish moss, and a collection of well-preserved historic homes. The square offers a peaceful escape for visitors looking to experience "old Savannah" architecture and serene landscaping away from the busier commercial areas.
attractions.address: 22 Habersham St
attractions.hours: Open 24/7
highlights: ['Historic Homes: Surrounded by beautifully preserved 18th and 19th-century residential architecture.', 'Spencer-Woodbridge House: Home to the oldest building on the square, dating back to 1790.', 'Tranquil Green Space: Features a well-maintained lawn and majestic live oaks perfect for relaxation.', 'Revolutionary War History: Named in honor of General Joseph Warren, a hero of the Battle of Bunker Hill.', 'Photographic Potential: A favorite spot for photographers seeking intimate, historic backdrops.']
attractions.tips: Visit during weekday mornings for the quietest atmosphere and optimal photography lighting. It is a perfect spot for a peaceful picnic or a break from exploring the busier parts of the historic district. Consider combining your visit with a walk to the nearby riverfront.
attractions.nearby: River Street, Bay Street, Washington Square, Reynolds Square, The Olde Pink House, Factors Walk

attractions.name: Wright Square
attractions.description: Wright Square, laid out in 1733, is one of the oldest and most historic of Savannah's 22 squares. It serves as a peaceful urban oasis characterized by its massive live oak trees and significant monuments, including a granite boulder marking the burial site of Yamacraw Indian Chief Tomochichi. Historically known as "Court House Square," it remains a central hub of the city's legal and civic life, surrounded by impressive architecture like the Tomochichi Federal Building and United States Court House.
attractions.address: Bull Street & President Street, Savannah, GA 31401
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (typical of Savannah city squares)
highlights: ["Tomochichi's Monument (a large granite boulder honoring the Yamacraw Indian Chief)", 'William Washington Gordon Monument (a tall granite and marble structure)', 'Massive historic live oak trees draped in Spanish moss', 'Surrounded by historic buildings including the Lutheran Church of the Ascension and the Old Chatham County Court House', 'Site of early colonial trials, auctions, and public announcements', 'Central location on Bull Street, ideal for walking tours and photography']
attractions.tips: Visit in the early morning for a peaceful experience before the crowds arrive. The square is a popular stop for ghost tours due to its haunted history, specifically the legend of Alice Riley. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the surrounding streets are part of Savannah's walkable historic grid. Pack a picnic to enjoy under the shade of the live oaks.
attractions.nearby: Juliette Gordon Low Birthplace, Johnson Square, Chippewa Square, Telfair Academy, Owens-Thomas House & Slave Quarters, Independent Presbyterian Church
